reason: Issue is well-scoped with clear acceptance criteria. The constants classes largely already exist in the codebase (HubRoutes, ProviderSettingKeys, CommentMarkers, AgentDefaults, GitConstants, PipelineConstants) and JWT deduplication is already complete. Remaining work is mechanical: update ~10 source files and ~25 test files to reference the existing constants instead of string literals, create the missing AgentEnvironmentVariables class, and add a few missing constants (timeouts, generic comment prefixes). No architectural decisions or behavioral changes are required.
concerns: Large diff surface (~35 files) increases risk of compile errors from typos; recommend batching by project and building after each batch.

AgentDefaults lives in the Pipeline project (namespace CodingAgentWebUI.Agent). Moving it to the Agent project could create circular references; safest to keep it where it is and create AgentEnvironmentVariables in the same project.
Razor components will need @using directives added to reference AgentDefaults constants cleanly.
plannedApproach: Create missing AgentEnvironmentVariables and AgentDefaults timeout constants, then mechanically replace hardcoded literals in source and test files with their corresponding constants, verifying with grep and dotnet test.
